```{r}
rq4a_mean_gamma_plot
```

This companion figure for RQ4a shows, for each substantive topic, how many bills it is the dominant topic for (the topic with the highest document–topic proportion), summarizing how often each topic serves as the primary frame in the corpus.

```{r}
#| label: rq4a-fig-dominant-topic
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-cap: "Figure RQ4a-ii. Number of bills for which each substantive topic has the highest document–topic proportion (dominant-topic assignment). Bills with tied maximum gamma are counted once for the first-ranked topic."

dominant_topic_counts <- readr::read_csv("data/rq4a_dominant_topic_counts.csv",
                                           show_col_types = FALSE)
BASE_THEME <- readRDS("data/plot_theme.rds")

rq4a_dom_plot <- dominant_topic_counts |>
  mutate(
    topic_label = str_wrap(topic_label, width = 28),
    topic_label = fct_reorder(topic_label, n_dominant_bills)
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = n_dominant_bills, y = topic_label)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#59a14f", width = 0.75) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = comma(n_dominant_bills)),
    hjust = -0.15, size = 3, color = "gray30"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18)),
    labels = comma
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Dominant-Topic Assignment Counts (RQ4a)",
    subtitle = "Number of bills where each topic has the highest \u03b3 \u00b7 substantive topics only",
    x        = "Number of Bills",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  BASE_THEME

ggsave("output/figures/rq4a_dominant_topic_counts.png",
       rq4a_dom_plot, width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 300)
rq4a_dom_plot
```

This chunk visualizes RQ4b by plotting, for three focal domains, the difference in mean document–topic proportion between high-domain bills and the full corpus for each substantive topic, so that topics over- or under-represented in high-domain bills stand out as blue or red cells.

```{r}
#| label: rq4b-fig-heatmap
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 7
#| fig-cap: "Figure RQ4b. Mean document–topic proportion (\u03b3) difference between high-domain bills (\u226575th percentile of focal domain score) and the full corpus, for each substantive topic and three focal SDOH domains. Positive values (blue) indicate over-representation of the topic in high-domain bills; negative values (red) indicate under-representation."

mean_gamma_diff <- readr::read_csv("data/rq4b_mean_gamma_diff.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
BASE_THEME <- readRDS("data/plot_theme.rds")

heatmap_data <- mean_gamma_diff |>
  mutate(
    topic_label_wrap = str_wrap(topic_label, width = 26),
    domain_label     = recode(
      domain,
      neighborhood_environment = "Neighborhood &\nBuilt Env.",
      education_access         = "Education\nAccess & Quality",
      discrimination_equity    = "Discrimination,\nEquity & Rights"
    )
  )

# Order topics by maximum absolute difference across focal domains
topic_order <- heatmap_data |>
  group_by(topic_label_wrap) |>
  summarise(max_abs = max(abs(gamma_diff)), .groups = "drop") |>
  arrange(max_abs) |>
  pull(topic_label_wrap)

heatmap_data <- heatmap_data |>
  mutate(topic_label_wrap = factor(topic_label_wrap, levels = topic_order))

rq4b_heatmap <- heatmap_data |>
  ggplot(aes(
    x    = domain_label,
    y    = topic_label_wrap,
    fill = gamma_diff
  )) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      label = ifelse(
        abs(gamma_diff) >= 0.005,
        sprintf("%+.3f", gamma_diff),
        ""
      ),
      color = abs(gamma_diff) > 0.012
    ),
    size = 2.9
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "#d1495b",
    mid      = "white",
    high     = "#2166ac",
    midpoint = 0,
    limits   = c(-0.03, 0.03),
    oob      = scales::squish,
    name     = "\u03b3 difference\n(high \u2212 corpus mean)"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(`TRUE` = "white", `FALSE` = "gray30"),
                     guide  = "none") +
  labs(
    title    = "Topic\u2013Domain Co-occurrence: Mean \u03b3 Difference in High-Domain Bills (RQ4b)",
    subtitle = "High-domain bills = \u226575th percentile of focal domain score \u00b7 topics dominated by boilerplate / citation tokens are excluded.",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  BASE_THEME +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid   = element_blank()
  )

ggsave("output/figures/rq4b_topic_domain_heatmap.png",
       rq4b_heatmap, width = 9, height = 7, dpi = 300)
rq4b_heatmap
```

This companion figure for RQ4b zooms in on the Education & Community topic and the Education Access & Quality domain, plotting bill-level document–topic proportions against education domain scores with a log-linear OLS smooth to show how topic prevalence increases with stronger education framing.

```{r}
#| label: rq4b-fig-scatter
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-cap: "Figure RQ4b-ii. Scatter plot of document–topic proportion (\u03b3) vs. Education Access & Quality domain score for the Education & Community topic. Smooth line is a log-linear OLS fit (y ~ log1p(x)); each point is one bill, jittered horizontally to reduce overplotting at integer domain scores."

topic_table_meta <- readr::read_csv("data/topic_table_meta.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
BASE_THEME <- readRDS("data/plot_theme.rds")

# Identify the best Education & Community × Education Access pairing
scatter_topic  <- "Education & Community"
scatter_domain <- "score_education_access"

scatter_data <- topic_table_meta |>
  filter(!is_artifact, topic_label == scatter_topic) |>
  select(bill_id, state, session_year, gamma,
         domain_score = all_of(scatter_domain))

rq4b_scatter <- scatter_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = domain_score, y = gamma)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = state),
              width = 0.25, height = 0, alpha = 0.45, size = 1.2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ log1p(x), se = TRUE,
              color = "#2166ac", fill = "#2166ac", alpha = 0.15,
              linewidth = 0.9) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired", name = "State") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = number_format(accuracy = 0.01)) +
  labs(
    title    = paste0("\u03b3 vs. Education Score: \"",
                      scatter_topic, "\" Topic (RQ4b)"),
    subtitle = "Log-linear OLS smooth (y ~ log1p(x)) with 95% CI \u00b7 jitter applied at integer domain score values",
    x        = "Education Access & Quality Domain Score (unique dict. matches)",
    y        = paste0("Document\u2013Topic Proportion (\u03b3): ",
                      scatter_topic)
  ) +
  BASE_THEME +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

ggsave("output/figures/rq4b_gamma_educ_scatter.png",
       rq4b_scatter, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
rq4b_scatter
```

This chunk addresses RQ4c by plotting mean document–topic proportions by state and topic, using a heatmap so that states where particular topics are especially prevalent (or rare) are visible as darker or lighter cells.

```{r}
#| label: rq4c-heatmap-state
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 7
#| fig-cap: "Figure RQ4c-i. Mean document\u2013topic proportion (\u03b3) by state and substantive topic. Each cell represents the mean \u03b3 across all bills introduced in that state. Rows are topics ordered by corpus-wide mean \u03b3; columns are the ten study states."

topic_prevalence <- readr::read_csv("data/rq4_topic_prevalence.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
topic_table_meta <- read_csv("data/topic_table_meta.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
BASE_THEME <- readRDS("data/plot_theme.rds")

state_topic_gamma <- topic_table_meta |>
  filter(!is_artifact, !is.na(state)) |>
  group_by(state, topic, topic_label) |>
  summarise(
    mean_gamma = mean(gamma, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_bills    = n_distinct(bill_id),
    .groups    = "drop"
  )

# Order topics by corpus-wide mean gamma (highest at top)
topic_order_corpus <- topic_prevalence |>
  filter(!is_artifact) |>
  arrange(desc(mean_gamma)) |>
  pull(topic_label)

# Wrap labels for readability
state_topic_heatmap_data <- state_topic_gamma |>
  mutate(
    topic_label_wrap = str_wrap(topic_label, width = 24),
    topic_label_wrap = factor(
      topic_label_wrap,
      levels = rev(str_wrap(topic_order_corpus, width = 24))
    )
  )

rq4c_state_heatmap <- state_topic_heatmap_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = state, y = topic_label_wrap, fill = mean_gamma)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      label = sprintf("%.3f", mean_gamma),
      color = mean_gamma > 0.09
    ),
    size = 2.5
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low  = "#f7fbff",
    high = "#08306b",
    name = "Mean \u03b3"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(`TRUE` = "white", `FALSE` = "gray30"),
    guide  = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "State \u00d7 Topic Prevalence Heatmap (RQ4c)",
    subtitle = "Mean document\u2013topic proportion (\u03b3) per state \u00b7 topics dominated by boilerplate / citation tokens are excluded.",
    x        = "State",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  BASE_THEME +
  theme(
    axis.text.x     = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid      = element_blank()
  )

ggsave("output/figures/rq4c_state_topic_heatmap.png",
       rq4c_state_heatmap, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)
rq4c_state_heatmap
```

This chunk continues RQ4c by plotting mean document–topic proportions over session years for the four SDOH‑focal topics, showing how the prevalence of each focal topic changes between 2021 and 2023.

```{r}
#| label: rq4c-year-trends
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-cap: "Figure RQ4c-ii. Mean document\u2013topic proportion (\u03b3) by session year for SDOH-focal topics (Education & Community; Insurance & Medicaid Coverage; Policy Equity & Conditions; Criminal Justice). Lines connect annual means across available session years. Only 2021 and 2023 appear because most states in the corpus hold biennial sessions; Florida contributes annual sessions but its bill count is too similar to other years to drive visible point-size variation."

topic_table_meta <- read_csv("data/topic_table_meta.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
BASE_THEME <- readRDS("data/plot_theme.rds")

sdoh_focal_topic_labels <- c(
  "Education & Community",
  "Insurance & Medicaid Coverage",
  "Policy Equity & Conditions",
  "Criminal Justice"
)

year_topic_gamma <- topic_table_meta |>
  filter(
    !is_artifact,
    !is.na(session_year),
    topic_label %in% sdoh_focal_topic_labels
  ) |>
  group_by(session_year, topic, topic_label) |>
  summarise(
    mean_gamma = mean(gamma, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_bills    = n_distinct(bill_id),
    .groups    = "drop"
  )

# Define a compact color palette for the four focal topics
FOCAL_TOPIC_COLORS <- c(
  "Education & Community"          = "#f28e2b",
  "Insurance & Medicaid Coverage"  = "#e15759",
  "Policy Equity & Conditions"     = "#b07aa1",
  "Criminal Justice"               = "#4e79a7"
)

rq4c_year_plot <- year_topic_gamma |>
  ggplot(aes(
    x     = session_year,
    y     = mean_gamma,
    color = topic_label,
    group = topic_label
  )) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.0) +
  geom_point(size = 3.5, alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_color_manual(values = FOCAL_TOPIC_COLORS, name = "Topic") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(2021, 2022, 2023, 2024)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = number_format(accuracy = 0.001)) +
  labs(
    title    = "SDOH-Focal Topic Prevalence Over Time (RQ4c)",
    subtitle = "Mean \u03b3 by session year \u00b7 point size = number of bills in that year",
    x        = "Session Year",
    y        = "Mean Document\u2013Topic Proportion (\u03b3)"
  ) +
  BASE_THEME +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

ggsave("output/figures/rq4c_year_topic_trends.png",
       rq4c_year_plot, width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 300)
rq4c_year_plot
```

This final RQ4c figure combines topic and domain measures by plotting, for three focal domains, each state’s mean document–topic proportion for the aligned topic (blue bars) alongside that state’s mean domain score (red points, rescaled), highlighting states where topic prevalence and domain scores move together.

```{r}
#| label: rq4c-fig-state-domain-interaction
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 9
#| echo: false
#| output: false
#| fig-cap: "Figure RQ4c-iii. State variation in topic--domain alignment for three focal SDOH domains. Blue bars = mean gamma of the aligned focal topic; red points = mean domain score across bills in that state. States with both a tall bar and a high red point show strong topic--domain alignment."

topic_table_meta <- read_csv("data/topic_table_meta.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
corpus_table  <- read_rds("data/corpus_table_scored.rds")
BASE_THEME <- readRDS("data/plot_theme.rds")

# Map each focal domain to its best-aligned topic (from RQ4b heatmap)
focal_topic_map <- tibble(
  domain      = c("education_access",
                  "discrimination_equity",
                  "neighborhood_environment"),
  topic_label = c("Education & Community",
                  "Policy Equity & Conditions",
                  "Criminal Justice"),
  domain_col  = c("score_education_access",
                  "score_discrimination_equity",
                  "score_neighborhood_environment"),
  domain_full = c("Education Access & Quality",
                  "Discrimination, Equity & Rights",
                  "Neighborhood & Built Environment")
)

# Build the combined state × focal-domain data frame
state_domain_interaction <- map_dfr(
  seq_len(nrow(focal_topic_map)),
  function(i) {
    row        <- focal_topic_map[i, ]
    score_col  <- row$domain_col

    # Mean gamma of the aligned topic per state
    gamma_state <- topic_table_meta |>
      filter(!is_artifact, topic_label == row$topic_label, !is.na(state)) |>
      group_by(state) |>
      summarise(mean_gamma = mean(gamma, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

    # Mean domain score per state
    score_state <- corpus_table |>
      filter(!is.na(state)) |>
      group_by(state) |>
      summarise(
        mean_score = mean(.data[[score_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups    = "drop"
      )

    left_join(gamma_state, score_state, by = "state") |>
      mutate(
        domain      = row$domain,
        topic_label = row$topic_label,
        domain_full = row$domain_full
      )
  }
)

# Normalize mean_score to the [0,1] range within each domain for dual-axis
# comparability (both gamma and scaled score plotted as bars/points on same scale)
state_domain_interaction <- state_domain_interaction |>
  group_by(domain) |>
  mutate(
    score_scaled = (mean_score - min(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE)) /
                   (max(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE) -
                    min(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE))
  ) |>
  ungroup()

# Compute a stable state order from the Education panel (highest mean_gamma)
# so all three facets share the same x-axis ordering
state_order_educ <- state_domain_interaction |>
  filter(domain == "education_access") |>
  arrange(mean_gamma) |>
  pull(state)

# Compute outside the plot
gamma_max <- max(state_domain_interaction$mean_gamma, na.rm = TRUE)

rq4c_interaction_plot <- state_domain_interaction |>
  mutate(
    state       = factor(state, levels = state_order_educ),
    domain_full = factor(domain_full,
                         levels = c("Education Access & Quality",
                                    "Discrimination, Equity & Rights",
                                    "Neighborhood & Built Environment"))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = state)) +
  geom_col(
    aes(y = mean_gamma),
    fill  = "#2166ac", alpha = 0.75, width = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(y = score_scaled * gamma_max),     # <-- use pre-computed scalar 
    color = "#d1495b", size = 3.5, shape = 16
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      xend  = state,
      y     = 0,
      yend  = score_scaled * gamma_max     # <-- same here
    ),
    color     = "#d1495b",
    linewidth = 0.7,
    alpha     = 0.5
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ domain_full, ncol = 1, scales = "fixed") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = number_format(accuracy = 0.001)) +
  labs(
    title    = "State x Focal-Domain Topic Alignment (RQ4c)",
    subtitle = "Blue bars = mean gamma of aligned focal topic · red points = mean domain score (normalized to gamma scale)",
    x        = "State",
    y        = "Mean gamma / Scaled Domain Score"
  ) +
  BASE_THEME +
  theme(
    axis.text.x     = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# Save to output folder
png("output/figures/rq4c_state_domain_interaction.png", width = 1200, height = 900, res = 100)
print(rq4c_interaction_plot)
dev.off()

# Save to figure-pdf folder for LaTeX inclusion
dir.create("project_code_template_files/figure-pdf", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
png("project_code_template_files/figure-pdf/rq4c-fig-state-domain-interaction-1.png",
    width = 1200, height = 900, res = 100)
print(rq4c_interaction_plot)
dev.off()
```

```{=latex}
\begin{figure}[H]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{project_code_template_files/figure-pdf/rq4c-fig-state-domain-interaction-1.png}
\caption{Figure RQ4c-iii. State variation in topic--domain alignment for three focal SDOH domains.}
\end{figure}
```
